title: [Epic] CLI
description: agentc CLI binary (Rust, agentc-cli crate). Record, traces, export, analyze, report, pricing, embed, migrate commands.

title: agentc embed --backfill + migrate CLI
description:
Implement maintenance CLI commands: embed --backfill and migrate.

**`agentc embed --backfill`:**
- Compute embeddings for spans with NULL input_embedding or output_embedding
- Reads content from input_content/output_content, decompresses zstd, extracts text (concatenated message content fields, newline-joined, text blocks only)
- Computes model2vec embedding (potion-base-8M, 256-dim) via Rust, stores as float16 BLOB (512 bytes)
- Skips spans whose content_id references are NULL (capture_content=False at capture time)
- Progress output: "Computed embeddings for N of M spans"

**`agentc migrate`:**
- Apply forward-compatible schema migrations to traces.db
- Checks PRAGMA user_version, applies ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN migrations
- Incompatible version → error: "Run agentc migrate to update your trace database."
- Displays migration summary: "Migrated traces.db from version {old} to {new}"

---

**Unit Tests:**
- embed --backfill computes embeddings for spans with NULL embeddings
- embed --backfill skips spans with NULL content_id
- embed --backfill decompresses zstd content correctly
- embed --backfill extracts text from structured JSON messages (text blocks only, excludes image/tool_use)
- embed --backfill stores float16 BLOB of correct size (512 bytes)
- embed --backfill is idempotent (re-run produces same embeddings, no duplicates)
- migrate applies ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN correctly
- migrate on current version is no-op
- migrate on incompatible version produces clear error message
- migrate preserves existing data

**Integration Tests:**
- Record with capture_content=True → embed --backfill fills embeddings → waste detectors now work
- Record with capture_content=False → embed --backfill skips all → no crash
- migrate on fresh DB → current schema version

**Logging:**
- INFO: "Computing embeddings for {n} spans with NULL embeddings"
- INFO: "Computed embeddings for {n} of {m} spans"
- WARN: "Skipping {n} spans with NULL content_id"
- INFO: "Migration applied: user_version {old} → {new}"
- INFO: "No migration needed (user_version = {current})"
- ERROR: "Incompatible schema version {v}. Run 'agentc migrate'."

title: PyO3/maturin scaffolding + FFI roundtrip
description:
Set up PyO3 bindings in agentc-profiler crate and verify roundtrip FFI calls between Python and Rust.

**Note:** pyproject.toml is defined in bd-3lb (workspace scaffolding bead). Not duplicated here.

**agentc-profiler crate:** Exports a #[pymodule] named _native. Initial exports:
- write_span(span_dict: dict) -> PyResult<()> — stub that validates dict keys exist
- A version string constant

**write_span stub specification:** `_native.write_span(span_dict: dict)` where span_dict keys are: `span_id`, `trace_id`, `parent_span_id`, `name`, `kind`, `start_time`, `end_time`, `status`, `model`, `provider`, `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `cache_creation_tokens`, `cache_read_tokens`, `attributes`, `input_messages`, `output_messages`.

**`_native.pyi` stub content:**
```python
def write_span(span_dict: dict) -> None: ...
def create_db(path: str) -> None: ...
def query_spans_by_trace(db_path: str, trace_id: str) -> list[dict]: ...
```

**Python side:** agentc/__init__.py imports from agentc._native. agentc/_native.pyi has type stubs matching the exports above.

**GIL release pattern:** All Rust functions that do CPU work use py.allow_threads(|| ...).

**Panic safety:** All #[pyfunction] use Result<T, PyErr> return types. Panic safety wrappers (Result<T, PyErr> on all #[pyfunction] exports) are implemented in this bead. Python wrapper catches BaseException at Rust call sites.

---

**Unit Tests (Rust):**
- #[cfg(test)] module: span_dict deserialization validates required keys (span_id, trace_id, name, kind, start_time)
- #[cfg(test)] module: missing required key → PyErr with descriptive message
- #[cfg(test)] module: optional keys (parent_span_id, model, provider) can be None/absent

**Unit Tests (Python):**
- import agentc._native succeeds
- agentc._native.__version__ returns a version string
- agentc._native.write_span({valid_dict}) returns without error
- agentc._native.write_span({missing_keys}) raises TypeError/ValueError with clear message
- agentc._native.write_span({}) raises error for missing required keys
- Type stubs (.pyi) match actual module exports (mypy --strict on test file)

**Integration Tests:**
- Full roundtrip: Python dict → Rust deserialization → stub processing → Python return
- GIL is released during CPU work (verify via concurrent Python threads)

**Logging:**
- DEBUG: "write_span called: span_id={span_id}, trace_id={trace_id}"
- ERROR: "write_span validation failed: missing key '{key}'"
design:
Standard maturin PyO3 setup. Module name `agentc._native` so users import `agentc`, never `_native` directly.

**Unit tests:** Rust-side: test `write_span` with valid and invalid dicts. Python-side: pytest calling `_native.write_span()` with mock data.
**E2E tests:** `maturin develop && pytest tests/test_ffi.py` in CI.
**Logging:** Rust panics logged via Python `agentc` logger.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `maturin develop` builds successfully
- [ ] `python -c "import agentc; print(agentc._native.__version__)"` prints version
- [ ] Roundtrip test: Python passes a dict to Rust, Rust validates and returns success
- [ ] `_native.pyi` type stubs exist and mypy accepts them
- [ ] `py.typed` marker present
